title: Doctor commands lack skill_agent traceability mapping
evidence: glob .opencode/skills/doctor-*/SKILL.md returns zero matches. No doctor-* skill directories exist. system-spec-kit/SKILL.md has no explicit doctor command ownership declaration.
scopeProof: glob doctor-*/SKILL.md x2 both 0 hits; grep system-spec-kit/SKILL.md for doctor ownership 0 hits. All 10 doctor commands unowned.
recommendation: Add explicit /doctor:* ownership declaration to system-spec-kit/SKILL.md or create stub doctor-* SKILL.md files.

title: No @doctor agent exists in any runtime directory
evidence: All 4 agent dirs have 11 agents each, none is @doctor. README.md:859 documents 11 agents. 5 new doctor commands (34 files) have no agent dispatch.
scopeProof: glob for @doctor* in all 4 agent dirs returns zero hits.
recommendation: Consider creating @doctor agent stub with cross-runtime mirroring for operator health checks. Defer to follow-on.

title: SC-001 claims 25 scenarios but only 23 exist (2 gapped per ADR-008)
evidence: SC-001 says '25 scenario .md files exist'. Spec body correctly says 23. ADR-008 documents DOC-337 + DOC-343 retired. ls returns 23 files.
scopeProof: ls 23--doctor-commands/*.md | wc -l returns 23; ADR-008 confirms intentional gaps.
recommendation: Update SC-001 from 25 to 23 with ADR-008 reference.

detail: 5 new doctor commands have no SKILL.md or explicit system-spec-kit ownership declaration. Commands exist at .opencode/commands/doctor/ but lack skill-level traceability.
No new @agent introduced by 013. All 4 runtime agent dirs in sync (11 agents + README.txt each). Root README.md correctly documents mirror policy.
All 23 scenarios exercise distinct capabilities across 5 doctor commands. Zero dead scenarios. 2 intentional gaps (DOC-337, DOC-343) documented in ADR-008.

reason: 1 open P1 (R7-P1-001: skill_agent mapping gap) blocks clean PASS. Agent mirroring clean. Playbook capability clean.
